fr.elements: Main Building; Front Driveway; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area; Patio Area
fr.considerations: Single Access Point; Dense Vegetation; Limited Rear Access
fr.recommendations: Enhancing responder access and capabilities at the property could be achieved by addressing several key considerations. Widening the single access point could facilitate more efficient entry and exit for emergency vehicles. Managing the dense vegetation around the access routes and building perimeter may enhance visibility and clearance, allowing responders to navigate more safely and quickly during an emergency situation. Improving access to the rear of the property, perhaps by developing an additional egress route, may support more effective emergency operations and staging for responders, ensuring that all areas of the property are easily reachable. Additionally, maintaining clear and unobstructed pathways around the vehicle parking and patio areas could further facilitate emergency personnel movement and staging, optimizing response times and operational efficiency.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Vegetated Perimeter; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetation Adjacent to Fence; Scattered Trees; Dense Tree Canopy
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing vegetation management around the property, particularly by increasing the distance between tree clusters and the structure, could help in creating a more effective defensible space. Reducing the continuity of the dense tree canopy and addressing the scattered trees to maintain open space areas would further diminish the potential for fire spread. Vegetation immediately adjacent to the structure and fence should be minimized to decrease direct exposure and reduce potential fuel sources close to improvements. This, in combination with efforts to trim or thin vegetation along the perimeter, could improve separation and support the creation of a perimeter buffer. Such actions would encourage better wildfire resilience and preparedness, while also supporting firefighter access and operations in the event of a wildfire.
